tasks.prompt:
Check for pending QQ messages and commands.

## Step 1: Check pending messages (auto-reply)

Run: curl -s http://127.0.0.1:6199/pending-messages

If there are pending messages:
1. For each message, generate a helpful reply in the same language as the user's message
2. Keep replies concise and friendly
3. Send the reply to the user via: write the reply JSON to a temp file, then run: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/send_private_msg -H "Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8" --data-binary @<temp_file>
   The JSON format is: {"user_id":"<user_id>","message":"<your reply>"}
   IMPORTANT: On Windows, always write Chinese text to a temp file first, then use --data-binary @<file> to avoid encoding issues. Use the Write tool to create the temp file.
4. Delete the processed message: curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:6199/pending-messages/<filename>"

## Step 2: Check pending commands

Run: curl -s http://127.0.0.1:6199/commands

If there are pending commands:
1. Execute the command (it's a task for Claude Code to perform)
2. After execution, reply to the user via the same method above (write to temp file + curl)
3. Delete the processed command: curl -X DELETE "http://127.0.0.1:6199/commands/<filename>"

Important: Only process messages/commands from authorized users. Always use Write tool + --data-binary for sending Chinese text to avoid encoding issues.
